economic_notes: Woodstock First Nation Economic Development Corporation includes Eagle's Nest and three retail fuel operations (Maliseet Fuels 1, Maliseet Fuels 2, Cardlock). WFN Cannabis also operates. Limited partnership with Wisokolamson Energy LP for wind power generation in Albert County NB - receives $100,000 or 2.4% of prior year income annually whichever is greater. Provincial tobacco/gasoline/motive fuel/HST tax sharing agreement expired January 2023 with no replacement yet - expected material impact on financial resources.
notes: Fiscal year end March 31, 2023. Province is New Brunswick. The Woodstock First Nation Economic Development Corporation (includes Eagle's Nest and three retail fuel operations) is fully consolidated. Economic Activities revenue of $54M dominates revenue - represents gas bar and cannabis own-source revenue. Provincial tax-sharing agreement (tobacco, gasoline, motive fuel, HST) expired January 2023 with no replacement - material risk to future revenues. Service Canada and DFO transfers included in revenue lines but not separately broken out in the government transfers schedule (Schedule 18 shows them combined under federal government transfers). Government transfers schedule shows additional transfers from Wolastoquey Tribal Council ($598,880) and First Nation Land Management Resource Centre ($18,665) and North Shore Mi'kmaq District Council ($32,562) not shown as separate revenue lines in the income statement - these appear embedded in the ISC or other lines. Remuneration schedule referenced on page 34 but not included in extracted text. Expenses total per income statement is $68,100,093 plus amortization $2,515,223 = $70,615,316 total. Prior year expenses similarly $58,859,294 + $2,391,676 = $61,250,970.
